problem_statement.title: feat: support multiple repo overrides
problem_statement.body:
This PR closes https://github.com/rstudio/renv/issues/716 to support setting named repos overrides via `RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE` in addition to the current single unnamed repo. 

Multiple repos can be specified via `RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE=NAME1=URL1;NAME2=URL2` where urls are semi-colon delimited.

Please let me know if I missed anything or if I can make any changes here!
problem_statement.issue_title: Feature request: multiple repos via renv.config.repos.override
problem_statement.issue_body: Setting multiple repos in the `renv.config.repos.override` option would be a great helper in case of managing multiple repositories

patch:
diff --git a/NEWS.md b/NEWS.md
index a3f071eea..e25c50d5e 100644
--- a/NEWS.md
+++ b/NEWS.md
@@ -1,6 +1,10 @@
 
 # renv (development version)
 
+* `RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE` now supports multiple named repositories using
+  the syntax `NAME1=URL1;NAME2=URL2`. Single repository URLs continue to be
+  supported.
+
 * Fixed an issue where `renv::sysreqs()` could fail on operating systems
   which don't declare a VERSION_ID in `/etc/os-release`. (#2197)
 
diff --git a/R/bootstrap.R b/R/bootstrap.R
index b3aaaca97..f602514e4 100644
--- a/R/bootstrap.R
+++ b/R/bootstrap.R
@@ -107,12 +107,20 @@ renv_bootstrap_repos <- function() {
   repos <- Sys.getenv("RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE", unset = NA)
   if (!is.na(repos)) {
 
-    # check for RSPM; if set, use a fallback repository for renv
-    rspm <- Sys.getenv("RSPM", unset = NA)
-    if (identical(rspm, repos))
-      repos <- c(RSPM = rspm, CRAN = cran)
+    # split on ';' if present
+    parts <- strsplit(repos, ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1L]]
 
-    return(repos)
+    # split into named repositories if present
+    idx <- regexpr("=", parts, fixed = TRUE)
+    keys <- substring(parts, 1L, idx - 1L)
+    vals <- substring(parts, idx + 1L)
+    names(vals) <- keys
+
+    # if we have a single unnamed repository, call it CRAN
+    if (length(vals) == 1L && identical(keys, ""))
+      names(vals) <- "CRAN"
+
+    return(vals)
 
   }
 
diff --git a/inst/config.yml b/inst/config.yml
index fcf459c80..af863fd15 100644
--- a/inst/config.yml
+++ b/inst/config.yml
@@ -268,6 +268,10 @@
     the usage of some set of repositories over what is defined in `renv.lock` or otherwise
     set by the R session.
 
+    This can be specified as a single repository URL (e.g., "https://cloud.r-project.org"),
+    or as multiple named repositories separated by semicolons using the format
+    NAME=URL (e.g., "CRAN=https://p3m.dev/cran/latest;R_UNIV=https://posit-dev.r-universe.dev").
+
 - name: "rspm.enabled"
   type: "logical[1]"
   default: true
diff --git a/inst/resources/activate.R b/inst/resources/activate.R
index 8b13aae8a..18d73e189 100644
--- a/inst/resources/activate.R
+++ b/inst/resources/activate.R
@@ -266,14 +266,22 @@ local({
     # check for repos override
     repos <- Sys.getenv("RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE", unset = NA)
     if (!is.na(repos)) {
-  
-      # check for RSPM; if set, use a fallback repository for renv
-      rspm <- Sys.getenv("RSPM", unset = NA)
-      if (identical(rspm, repos))
-        repos <- c(RSPM = rspm, CRAN = cran)
-  
-      return(repos)
-  
+
+      # split on ';' if present
+      parts <- strsplit(repos, ";", fixed = TRUE)[[1L]]
+
+      # split into named repositories if present
+      idx <- regexpr("=", parts, fixed = TRUE)
+      keys <- substring(parts, 1L, idx - 1L)
+      vals <- substring(parts, idx + 1L)
+      names(vals) <- keys
+
+      # if we have a single unnamed repository, call it CRAN
+      if (length(vals) == 1L && identical(keys, ""))
+        names(vals) <- "CRAN"
+
+      return(vals)
+
     }
   
     # check for lockfile repositories
test_patch:
diff --git a/tests/testthat/test-bootstrap.R b/tests/testthat/test-bootstrap.R
index 7b805208b..2842d876d 100644
--- a/tests/testthat/test-bootstrap.R
+++ b/tests/testthat/test-bootstrap.R
@@ -284,3 +284,47 @@ test_that("bootstrap version validation handles 'standard' remote types", {
   )
 
 })
+
+# repos override tests ----------------------------------------------------
+
+test_that("repos override supports unnamed repo", {
+  renv_tests_scope()
+  url <- "https://p3m.dev/cran/latest"
+  renv_scope_envvars(RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE = url)
+  repos <- renv_bootstrap_repos()
+  expect_true(url %in% repos)
+})
+
+test_that("repos override can use 1 named repository", {
+  renv_tests_scope()
+  override <- "CRAN=https://p3m.dev/cran/latest"
+  renv_scope_envvars(RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE = override)
+  repos <- renv_bootstrap_repos()
+  expect_equal(repos["CRAN"], c(CRAN = "https://p3m.dev/cran/latest"))
+})
+
+test_that("repos override can use 2+ named repositories", {
+  renv_tests_scope()
+  override <- "CRAN=https://p3m.dev/cran/latest;R_UNIV=https://posit-dev.r-universe.dev"
+  renv_scope_envvars(RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE = override)
+  repos <- renv_bootstrap_repos()
+  expect_equal(repos["CRAN"], c(CRAN = "https://p3m.dev/cran/latest"))
+  expect_equal(repos["R_UNIV"], c(R_UNIV = "https://posit-dev.r-universe.dev"))
+  expect_equal(length(repos), 2L)
+})
+
+test_that("repos override can handle `=` in repo urls", {
+  renv_tests_scope()
+  override <- "R_UNIV=https://posit-dev.r-universe.dev/?example=query"
+  renv_scope_envvars(RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE = override)
+  repos <- renv_bootstrap_repos()
+  expect_equal(repos["R_UNIV"], c(R_UNIV = "https://posit-dev.r-universe.dev/?example=query"))
+})
+
+test_that("repos override handle paths", {
+  renv_tests_scope()
+  override <- "CRAN=https://p3m.dev/cran/__linux__/noble/latest"
+  renv_scope_envvars(RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE = override)
+  repos <- renv_bootstrap_repos()
+  expect_equal(repos["CRAN"], c(CRAN = "https://p3m.dev/cran/__linux__/noble/latest"))
+})

fail_to_pass: repos override supports unnamed repo - single URL should be named CRAN
repos override can use 1 named repository - NAME=URL format works
repos override can use 2+ named repositories - semicolon-delimited NAME=URL pairs work
repos override can handle `=` in repo urls - URLs with query parameters containing = are parsed correctly
repos override handle paths - URLs with path separators work correctly
pass_to_pass: Existing bootstrap version validation tests continue to pass
Other bootstrap repository resolution logic remains unchanged when RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE is not set

key_changes: Split RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE value on ';' using strsplit()
Use regexpr() with fixed=TRUE to find first '=' in each part
Use substring() to extract name (before '=') and URL (after '=')
Assign names to the values vector using names(vals) <- keys
Handle edge case: single unnamed URL should be named 'CRAN'
Apply same changes to both R/bootstrap.R and inst/resources/activate.R
potential_pitfalls: Using strsplit() on '=' would fail for URLs containing '=' in query parameters - must use regexpr() to find only the first '='
Forgetting to handle the single unnamed URL case (should default to 'CRAN')
Not updating both R/bootstrap.R and inst/resources/activate.R (they contain parallel implementations)
Empty keys when no '=' is present - need to check with identical(keys, "")

instruction: Implement support for multiple named repositories in the `RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE` environment variable. The function `renv_bootstrap_repos()` in R/bootstrap.R should parse the environment variable to support: (1) a single unnamed URL which gets named 'CRAN', and (2) multiple named repositories in the format `NAME1=URL1;NAME2=URL2` where repositories are semicolon-delimited. The same logic must also be implemented in inst/resources/activate.R which contains a parallel implementation for the activation script.
context:
The renv_bootstrap_repos() function currently reads RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE and returns it as-is. The new implementation should:

1. Split the value on ';' to get individual repo specifications
2. For each part, split on the first '=' to get NAME=URL
3. If a single unnamed URL is provided (no '='), name it 'CRAN'
4. Return a named character vector of repositories

Key function signature:
r
renv_bootstrap_repos <- function() {
  repos <- Sys.getenv("RENV_CONFIG_REPOS_OVERRIDE", unset = NA)
  # ... parsing logic ...
  return(vals)  # named character vector
}
